description: CLI interface for Progress ServiceNow Employee Center (browser automation)

auth.usage_instructions: Manage browser-session authentication. Use `auth login` to start a browser session (use --force/-F to re-authenticate). Use `auth status` to check current auth state. Use `auth test` to verify credentials work.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Start authentication. Use --force/-F to clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate. Use --profile/-p to save to a named profile. Use --credential-type/-c to authenticate only a specific credential type.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions. Use --profile/-p to target a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage named authentication profiles for switching between environments. Use `auth profiles list` to see all profiles, `auth profiles set-default` to change the active profile.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new named profile (positional argument, required). Creates from the .env.example template. Then use `auth login --profile <name>` to configure credentials.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile by name (positional argument, required). Use --force/-F to skip confirmation prompt.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific profile by name (positional argument, required). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all authentication profiles with their default status. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --filter/-f to filter (e.g., "is_default:eq:True").

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).
status.usage_instructions: Check whether the CLI is currently authenticated. Returns JSON with "authenticated": true/false. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Test that stored credentials actually work by making a live verification request. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage the local response cache. Use `cache clear` to remove all cached responses and free disk space.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached responses from the local cache directory. Reports how many files and bytes were freed.

catalog.usage_instructions: Browse and search the ServiceNow service catalog. Use `catalog list` to browse by category (it, business-operations, workplace-operations), `catalog search` for keyword search, `catalog get` for item details.

get.help: Get details for a specific catalog item by sys_id.
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for a catalog item by its sys_id. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

list.help: List catalog items, optionally filtered by category.
list.usage_instructions: List catalog items. Use --category/-C to filter by category: it, business-operations, workplace-operations, or a topic_id. Default limit is 100. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --category
options.help: Category: it, business-operations, workplace-operations, or a topic_id

search.usage_instructions: Search the catalog by keyword query (positional argument, required). Use --limit/-l to control result count (default 100). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

ticket.usage_instructions: Ticket operations — listing, viewing, commenting on, closing, and creating ServiceNow RITM requests. Use `ticket list` to see open/closed/watchlist tickets, `ticket get` for details on a specific RITM. Use `ticket template list/get/fields` for offline form schemas (no auth needed).

close.usage_instructions: Close a ticket by RITM number or sys_id. No confirmation prompt — closes immediately.

comment.help: Post a comment on a ticket.
examples: progress-servicenow ticket comment RITM0352332 "Please update the status"
progress-servicenow ticket comment abc123... "Thank you for the update"
comment.usage_instructions: Post a comment on a ticket. Requires RITM number (or sys_id) and the comment message text as positional arguments.

create.help: Create a ServiceNow ticket. Without --template, opens a headed browser for manual ticket creation. With --template, creates a ticket programmatically by filling in the catalog item form via browser automation and submitting it.
examples: progress-servicenow ticket create --template development_cloud_issue --field product=Azure --field description="Request to create Document Intelligence resource"
progress-servicenow ticket create -T development_cloud_issue -F product=Azure -F impact=High
create.usage_instructions: Opens a headed browser window at the ServiceNow portal for manual ticket creation. Use when you need to fill out a catalog request form interactively.

options.name: --template
options.help: Template key for programmatic creation (e.g., 'development_cloud_issue'). Use 'ticket template list' to see all keys

options.name: --field
options.help: Field key=value pair. Repeatable. Use 'ticket template fields <key>' to see available fields

options.name: --dry-run
options.help: Validate fields against the template without submitting

options.name: --draft
options.help: Save as draft instead of submitting

form.help: Inspect live catalog item forms (discover fields, search lookups)
form.usage_instructions: Live browser-backed form introspection. Use when ticket_template.json has an empty or stale `fields: {}` block, or when you need to know the exact value of a Select2/reference field (e.g., application name) before creating a ticket. Unlike `template fields`, these hit the real form via the authenticated browser.

inspect.help: Inspect the live fields on a catalog item form. Opens the form in the authenticated browser, reads the accessibility tree, and returns every form field as a JSON record with its label, type (dropdown/reference/textarea/checkbox/text), required flag, and a snake_case key suggestion suitable for ticket_template.json. Use this when a template's `fields: {}` block is empty or stale, or when you want to confirm the real field structure before creating a ticket.
inspect.usage_instructions: Navigates to a catalog item form, reads the accessibility tree, and returns every form field with label, type (dropdown/reference/textarea/checkbox/text), required flag, and a snake_case key suggestion. Provide exactly one of --template or --url.

options.name: --url
options.help: Direct catalog item form URL. Mutually exclusive with --template

lookup.help: Search a Select2/reference field on a catalog item form. Opens the form, clicks the named field, types the search string, and returns the matching option labels from the dropdown. Use this to find the exact value to pass to a reference field when creating a ticket (e.g., the exact application name in Request Application Assistance).
lookup.usage_instructions: Navigates to a catalog item form, finds the dropdown/reference field by its visible label, types the search string, and returns the matching option labels. Use this to find the exact value to pass to a reference field when creating a ticket. Provide exactly one of --template or --url.

options.name: --field
options.help: Exact label of the dropdown/reference field to search (e.g., 'Please select the application from the list')

options.name: --search
options.help: Search string to type into the lookup (e.g., 'Copilot')

options.name: --url
options.help: Direct catalog item form URL. Mutually exclusive with --template

get.help: Get details for a specific ticket.
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for a specific ticket by RITM number (e.g., RITM0352332) or sys_id. Use --comments/-c to include activity comments. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.help: List tickets from My Requests page.
list.usage_instructions: List tickets from the My Requests page. Use --view/-V to switch between open, closed, watchlist-open (default), watchlist-closed. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Default limit is 100.

product.help: Product field options for catalog items
product.usage_instructions: Run `progress-servicenow ticket product --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get one configured Product dropdown option by exact name.
get.usage_instructions: Run `progress-servicenow ticket product get --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List configured Product dropdown options from packaged template metadata.
list.usage_instructions: Lists Product dropdown options configured in the packaged ticket template metadata. Returns an empty list when no Product options are configured.

template.help: Ticket form templates (offline — no auth required)
template.usage_instructions: Offline access to catalog item form schemas. Use BEFORE creating a ticket to know exactly which fields are available. No browser session or authentication needed.

fields.help: List all form fields for a specific catalog item template. Shows field names, types, required status, defaults, placeholders, and help text for every field on the form.
fields.usage_instructions: Shows field names, types, required status, defaults, options, and help text. Use --required/-r to show only required fields.

get.help: Get the full template for a specific catalog item. Returns the catalog item metadata and all its form fields with types, defaults, placeholders, help text, and required status.
get.usage_instructions: Returns catalog item metadata and all form fields. Use the key from `ticket template list`.

list.help: List all available catalog item templates. Shows every catalog item that can be used to create a ticket, along with its key, description, category, and number of documented fields.
list.usage_instructions: Lists every catalog item that can be used to create a ticket, with id/key, name, description, category, field count, and required fields. No auth required.

usage_instructions: Use for interacting with Progress ServiceNow Employee Center — managing tickets (RITM requests), browsing the service catalog, and handling authentication. Commands follow `progress-servicenow <group> <action>` pattern. This is a browser-automation CLI; auth uses a browser session. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p. IMPORTANT: Before creating a ticket, ALWAYS use `template list` and `template fields <key>` to discover available catalog items and their form fields — this is offline (no auth) and avoids unnecessary browser lookups.
